mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) signals expanding factory output, which raises corporate earnings expectations and prompts re-rating of equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 3-month rolling correlation between INDPRO growth and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is not significantly positive (p>0.05) over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) drops below 5.0%, the labor market becomes tight enough to generate wage pressure, compressing corporate margins and triggering negative forward returns over the next 60 trading days as inflation fears weigh on equity valuations.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return after UNRATE falls below 5.0 is not negative by more than -1% or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates both strong consumer demand and accommodative monetary policy, amplifying the positive effect on equity prices through a demand-pull and discount-rate channel over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS decline in a linear regression predicting 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p>0.10) or the conditional long-short strategy has Sharpe ratio below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of above-trend GDP growth (GDPC1 year-over-year growth exceeding 3%), rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals capacity constraints that stoke inflation fears, causing the S&P 500 to decline over the subsequent 60 trading days as the market prices in tighter monetary policy.
failure_condition: During periods of GDP growth above 3%, the correlation between INDPRO growth and 60-day forward returns is not negative, or the conditional Sharpe ratio of the short signal is below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: A one-month increase in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects immediate consumer strength that lifts short-term earnings sentiment, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as traders front-run expected improvement in corporate sales.
failure_condition: The 1-month change in RSAFS has no significant positive rank correlation (Rank IC < 0.02) with subsequent 5-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above 0.25%, the market interprets a shift toward tightening that raises borrowing costs and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as future cash flows are discounted more heavily.
failure_condition: After a FEDFUNDS level above 0.25%, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the proportion of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and increasing federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals an overheated labor market prompting monetary tightening, which contracts equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as policy expectations sour.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and FEDFUNDS change in a regression predicting 5-day returns is not significantly negative (p>0.10) or the joint signal fails to produce a negative mean return over a 2-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary easing cycle (defined by a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS), the federal funds rate is at a historically low level, further compressing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods where the 3-month change in FEDFUNDS is negative, the 20-day forward return is not significantly positive (p>0.05) or the rolling Rank IC stays below 0.04 for more than 2 consecutive years.
